title: Cephalantheropsis venusta
taxonomy: Cephalantheropsis venusta is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. Further, it is placed in the subfamily Epidendroideae and the tribe Cranichideae. The genus Cephalantheropsis is a part of the subtribe Gynopodium. Cephalantheropsis venusta is also a member of the order Asparagales.

intro: Cephalantheropsis venusta, commonly known as the Venus lip orchid, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to China. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional Chinese medicine in treating various ailments, such as fever and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cephalantheropsis venusta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into various proprietary herbal formulations.
